overrides.description: Generates a movie trivia quiz for one film and caches it on the movieQuizzes doc for reuse. Send `movieTitle` and `movieId`; returns the question set. Writes to movieQuizzes. Do not call it to fetch an existing quiz — use get-shared-questions for that; a call here can spend a model generation. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.

Records one completed quiz attempt for the signed-in user and rolls the derived state forward. Send `sessionId`, `answers`, `movieId`, `movieTitle` and `movieData`; returns the scored result. Writes a quizAttempts doc and updates watched and unlockedLists, so calling it twice for one session double-counts. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.

Flags one quiz question as wrong or broken for human review. Send `movieId`, `movieTitle`, `question`, `options`, `correctAnswer` and `questionIndex`; returns ok. Writes a report and updates the movieQuizzes doc. Not for editing a question — that is manage-question. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.

Marks a movie as watched for the signed-in user, which can unlock list badges as a side effect. Send the `movie` object; returns 201 on the write. Writes to watched and unlockedLists. Do not use it to build a wishlist — that is add-to-want-to-see. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.

Records the signed-in user's rating of one movie. Send `movieId` and `score`; returns 201. Writes to movies and ratings. Not the quiz score path — quiz results go through save-quiz-result. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.
Adds a movie to the signed-in user's want-to-see list. Send the `movie` object; returns 201. Writes to wantToSee only, never to watched. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.

Returns the signed-in user's profile document. Read-only aside from a rate-limit counter, and it 429s when you hammer it, so do not poll it in a loop. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.

Returns the ranked user leaderboard, filterable by `locationId`, `locationRegion` and `limit` in the query string. Read-only. For one user's own position use get-my-rank instead of paging this. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.

Returns the signed-in user's own leaderboard position without paging the whole board. Read-only apart from a rate-limit counter; 429s under repeat calls. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.

Creates a screening event. Send `movieTitle`, `movieId`, `eventDate`, `eventTime`, `locationName`, `locationCity` and `locationRegion`; returns 201 with the new event. Writes a screeningEvents doc. Despite the plural name this is a POST that creates — it does not list events; get-admin-events does. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.

Verifies a user's ticket for a screening event and credits attendance. Send `eventId` and `movieId`; returns 200 with the verification. Updates screeningEvents, watched and the user doc. Never call it speculatively — it grants credit. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.
Returns platform-wide counts over users and screeningEvents for the admin dashboard. Read-only. Requires a Firebase ID token AND an admin role on the user doc; non-admins get 403.

Returns the user directory for admin review, filtered by `search` and `limit` in the query string. Read-only. Not for a user reading their own record — that is get-user-profile. Requires a Firebase ID token AND an admin role on the user doc; non-admins get 403.

Changes one user's role, which is a privilege escalation path. Send the target user in the path and the new `role` in the body; returns 200. Updates the users doc. Never call it to update anything but the role, and never on behalf of a user acting on themselves. Requires a Firebase ID token AND an admin role on the user doc; non-admins get 403.

Changes one user's assigned location. Send `locationId`, `locationName`, `locationCity` and `locationRegion`; returns 200. Updates the users doc. For a user setting their own location the first time, use set-initial-location. Requires a Firebase ID token AND an admin role on the user doc; non-admins get 403.
Returns screening events for admin review, filtered by `status` in the query string. Read-only. Requires a Firebase ID token AND an admin role on the user doc; non-admins get 403.

Activates or deactivates one screening event. Send `active`; returns 200. Updates the screeningEvents doc; deactivating hides the event from users. Requires a Firebase ID token AND an admin role on the user doc; non-admins get 403.

Returns the recent public quiz-attempt feed, capped by `limit` in the query string. Read-only. Open: the handler runs no auth check, so it answers any caller. Do not pass anything user-identifying to it.

Returns the on-this-day cinema feature for the current date, served from the dailyCinemaCache and refilled on a miss. Read-only to callers; a cache miss writes the cache doc. Open: the handler runs no auth check, so it answers any caller.

Returns the signed-in user's own quiz attempts, capped by `limit`. Read-only. Never use it to read another user's history — it is scoped to the token holder. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.
Returns aggregate quiz analytics across quizAttempts for the admin dashboard. Read-only. Requires a Firebase ID token AND an admin role on the user doc; non-admins get 403.

Returns the signed-in user's own movie lists and their unlock state. Read-only to the caller; may materialize the unlockedLists doc on first read. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.

Creates or overwrites one of the signed-in user's movie lists. Send `listName`, `description`, `movies` and `isPublic`; returns 200. Writes the userLists doc and 403s when the list type is not unlocked. It OVERWRITES: send the whole `movies` array, not a delta, or you will drop entries.
Returns how close the signed-in user is to unlocking each list type. Read-only. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.

Returns one user's public lists by user id. Read-only. Open: the handler runs no auth check, so it answers any caller. Never use it to read private lists — it only serves what the owner marked public, and get-my-lists is the authenticated path.
Returns the public community list feed, filtered by `listType`, `sort` and `limit`. Read-only. Open: the handler runs no auth check, so it answers any caller.

Toggles the signed-in user's like on one community list. Returns 200 with the new state. Writes listLikes and updates the userLists like count — it TOGGLES, so a repeat call unlikes. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.

Generates a badge icon image from a `prompt` for `badgeId` and stores it. Returns the asset. Writes badgeAssets and a rateLimits counter; 429s when the caller is over quota, and each call spends an image generation. Do not call it to fetch an existing badge — use list-badge-assets or get-legacy-badges. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.
Returns the badge assets already generated. Read-only. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.
Returns the badges earned by the user named in `userId`. Read-only. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.

Creates a fantasy-league theater for the signed-in manager. Send `theaterName`, `storeId` and `regionId`; returns 201. Writes theaters and seeds a managerStats doc. One theater per manager — do not call it to rename an existing one. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.
Returns the caller's theater with its current weekend lineup and manager stats, or 404 when no theater exists yet. Read-only. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.
Returns the movies draftable for the weekend named in `weekendDate`. Read-only. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.

Saves the weekend screen lineup for a theater. Send `theaterId` and `screens`; returns the saved lineup. Writes weekendLineups and 403s when the caller does not own the theater. It replaces the whole lineup, and it will not touch a lineup already locked by lock-lineup. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.
Returns the fantasy-league configuration constants the client needs to render the draft. Read-only, no user data. Open: the handler runs no auth check, so it answers any caller.
Returns the fantasy manager standings, scoped by `weekendDate`, `type`, `scope`, `storeId` and `regionId`. Read-only. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.

Locks a theater's weekend lineup so it can no longer be edited. Send `theaterId`; returns 200. Updates the weekendLineups doc and 403s for a non-owner. IRREVERSIBLE for the weekend — there is no unlock route, so never call it to "save" a draft.

Scores every theater's lineup for `weekendDate` and writes the results back. Returns the scoring summary. Updates weekendLineups and theaters across ALL managers — this is a league-wide batch job, not a per-user read. Run it once per weekend; a second run rescores and overwrites. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.

Loads the weekend box-office numbers used for scoring. Send `weekendDate` and the `movies` array; returns ok. Writes the weekendBoxOffice doc, overwriting whatever was there for that weekend. Despite the name ending in "Data" this is a POST that writes — get-box-office-data is the read. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.
Returns the stored box-office numbers for `weekendDate`. Read-only. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.

Rescores the league for `weekendDate` against the ingested real box-office numbers rather than projections. Updates weekendLineups and theaters league-wide. Requires ingest-box-office-data to have run for that weekend first; a second run overwrites the prior scoring. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.

Generates and stores trivia questions for a `category` at a `difficulty`, up to `limit`. Returns the count written. Writes triviaQuestions and can 502 when the upstream model fails; each call spends a generation, so never use it as a read. Requires a Firebase ID token AND an admin role on the user doc; non-admins get 403.
Returns stored trivia questions filtered by `category`, `difficulty`, `personality` and `limit`. Read-only. Open: the handler runs no auth check, so it answers any caller.

Rewrites existing questions named in `questionIds` into a `personality` voice, up to `limit`. Returns the variations and updates the triviaQuestions docs. Spends a model generation per call. Requires a Firebase ID token AND an admin role on the user doc; non-admins get 403.
Returns the list of trivia personality voices available. Read-only, static config. Open: the handler runs no auth check, so it answers any caller.

Batch-parses up to `limit` raw knowledge entries into structured trivia questions; `overwrite` replaces existing parses. Writes triviaQuestions. A long-running admin batch — never call it inline in a user request path. Requires a Firebase ID token AND an admin role on the user doc; non-admins get 403.
Returns the knowledge-base entry for a film `title`, up to `limit` results, or 404 when unknown. Read-only. Open: the handler runs no auth check, so it answers any caller.
Returns the knowledge-base entry for a TV `title`, up to `limit` results, or 404 when unknown. Read-only. Open: the handler runs no auth check, so it answers any caller.
Searches film and TV knowledge for the query `q`, narrowed by `type` and `limit`. Read-only. Open: the handler runs no auth check, so it answers any caller.
Returns coverage counts over the knowledge base, served from gameCache. Read-only to callers; refills the cache doc on a miss. Open: the handler runs no auth check, so it answers any caller.

Adds one curated question to the knowledge base. Send `title`, `type`, `question`, `correctAnswer`, `incorrectAnswers` and `difficulty`; returns 201. Writes the knowledge doc. For a user-contributed question that still needs review, use submit-question. Requires a Firebase ID token AND an admin role on the user doc; non-admins get 403.

Returns trivia questions that match no film or TV knowledge entry, up to `limit`, for admin triage. Read-only to the caller. Requires a Firebase ID token AND an admin role on the user doc; non-admins get 403.

Attaches the orphaned question `questionId` to the knowledge entry named by `title` and `type`. Returns 200 and updates the triviaQuestions doc. 404s when the question is gone. Requires a Firebase ID token AND an admin role on the user doc; non-admins get 403.

Submits a user-authored trivia question for review. Send `title`, `type`, `question`, `correctAnswer`, `incorrectAnswers`, `difficulty` and `source`; returns 201. Writes questionSubmissions — it does NOT publish; review-submission does. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.
Returns pending question submissions filtered by `status` and `limit`. Read-only. Requires a Firebase ID token AND an admin role on the user doc; non-admins get 403.

Approves or rejects the submission `submissionId` via `action`, with `rejectionReason` on a reject. Returns 200. Approving publishes the question into the bank; rejecting is not reversible from the API. Requires a Firebase ID token AND an admin role on the user doc; non-admins get 403.

Reports a problem with a knowledge entry or question. Send `questionId`, `title`, `type`, `issueType` and `description`; returns 201. Writes knowledgeIssues; it does not change the question. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.
Returns reported knowledge issues filtered by `status` and `limit`. Read-only. Requires a Firebase ID token AND an admin role on the user doc; non-admins get 403.

Closes the issue `issueId` with `action` and `resolution`, and optionally sets `deleteQuestion` to remove the underlying question. Returns 200. DESTRUCTIVE when `deleteQuestion` is true — the question is gone and no route restores it. Requires a Firebase ID token AND an admin role on the user doc; non-admins get 403.

Records per-question correctness stats after a quiz. Send `movieId` and `questionResults`; returns 200. Updates the movieQuizzes doc. Called by the quiz flow — do not call it by hand, it skews the shared question bank. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.
Returns question-bank health for `movieId`: how many questions exist and how they perform. Read-only. Open: the handler runs no auth check, so it answers any caller.

Returns cached questions for a movie so a quiz can be served without generating one. Filter with `movieTitle`, `movieId`, `count`, `minCorrectRate` and `isKidMode`. Prefer this over generate-quiz whenever a cached set exists — it costs nothing. Bumps a serve counter on the movieQuizzes doc. Open: the handler runs no auth check, so it answers any caller.

Returns the active challenge list. DECLARES a token requirement, but the last verify run answered an unauthenticated caller (auth-gate-open) — treat this route as PUBLIC until that is fixed, and never assume the gate holds. Read-only.
Returns the signed-in user's own challenge progress. Read-only. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.

Enrolls the signed-in user in the challenge `challengeId`. Returns 200 and writes a userChallenges doc; 404s when the challenge is gone. Joining is not undoable through the API. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.

Claims the reward for the completed challenge `challengeId`. Returns 200, updates userChallenges and credits the users doc. Only call it once a challenge is genuinely complete — it grants points. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.

Creates a challenge from `templateId` with `type`, `startDate`, `endDate`, `featured` and any `overrides`. Returns 200 and writes the challenges doc, which makes it live for every user. Requires a Firebase ID token AND an admin role on the user doc; non-admins get 403.
Returns the challenge templates available to create-challenge. Read-only. Requires a Firebase ID token AND an admin role on the user doc; non-admins get 403.
Returns users awaiting approval. Read-only. Requires a Firebase ID token AND an admin role on the user doc; non-admins get 403.

Approves the waitlisted user `userId` and assigns `role`, `locationId`, `locationName`, `locationCity` and `locationRegion`. Returns 200 and updates the users doc, which grants them access to the app. Requires a Firebase ID token AND an admin role on the user doc; non-admins get 403.

Rejects the waitlisted user `userId` with a `reason`. Returns 200 and updates the users doc, denying them access. There is no un-reject route. Requires a Firebase ID token AND an admin role on the user doc; non-admins get 403.

Sets the signed-in user's location the first time they onboard. Send `locationId`, `locationName`, `locationCity` and `locationRegion`; returns 200. Updates the caller's own users doc and 429s when repeated — for an admin changing someone else's location, use update-user-location. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.

Saves one completed Bacon-trail game run. Send `startingActorId`, `startingActorName`, `steps`, `trail`, `won` and `date`; returns 200. Writes a baconTrailResults doc and updates the user. One write per finished run — replaying it inflates the leaderboard. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.
Returns the Bacon-trail standings for `date`, capped by `limit`. Read-only. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.
Returns actors with today's birthday, capped by `limit` and optionally narrowed by `requireBaconNumber`. Read-only. Open: the handler runs no auth check, so it answers any caller.
Returns coverage counts over the actor database. Read-only. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.

Returns a tagline-game session for the signed-in user, served from gameCache. Read-only to the caller; materializes the session doc on a miss, so two calls can hand back two different sessions. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.
Records the result of a tagline-game session. Send `score`, `totalQuestions` and `results`; returns 200. One submission per session. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.
Returns the archived legacy badge assets for `badgeId`. Read-only. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.
Restores the archived legacy asset `legacyId` back onto `badgeId`. Returns 200 and writes badgeAssets, replacing the badge's current image. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.
Returns the question bank for `movieId`, or the movies matching `search`, for admin curation. Read-only. Requires a Firebase ID token AND an admin role on the user doc; non-admins get 403.

Edits or removes one question in a movie's bank. Send `movieId`, `questionId`, `action` and `updates`; returns 200. Updates the movieQuizzes doc; a delete `action` is not reversible. Requires a Firebase ID token AND an admin role on the user doc; non-admins get 403.

Creates an invite for `email` with the given `role`. Returns 200 and writes an invites doc, which lets that address register. Requires a Firebase ID token AND an admin role on the user doc; non-admins get 403.

Generates the anagram set for the film `title` used by the word game. Returns the generated batch and 403s outside the allowed caller. Each call spends a generation — do not use it as a read. Requires a Firebase ID token in `Authorization: Bearer`.

affordances.description: Dev-only: seed one test user (agent-test-user), three quizAttempts docs, and one pointHistory row with fixed ids for agent-driven verification. 404s outside the emulator/dev gate.

overrides.description: Dev-only: seeds one test user (agent-test-user), three quizAttempts docs, and one pointHistory row with fixed ids so an agent can verify against known data. 404s outside the emulator/dev gate, and it is never reachable in prod. Not for use against real user data.
